task_completion.description: Navigate to the Talon user_commands directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: File Explorer or terminal showing the directory C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\community\user_commands containing the .talon files.

task_completion.description: Open and inspect the .talon files for duplicate triggers
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., VS Code, Notepad) showing the contents of editor_commands.talon, general_commands.talon, or productivity_commands.talon, revealing the voice triggers.

task_completion.description: Comment out or remove the less specific duplicate commands
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text editor showing the duplicate triggers ('go to line', 'open terminal', 'save file') being commented out (typically with a '#' symbol) or deleted, leaving only the most specific version active.
A text editor creating or saving a new file specifically named 'audit_report.txt' in the user_commands directory.

task_completion.description: Document the conflicts and resolutions in the audit report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of audit_report.txt showing documentation of the 3 duplicate triggers ('go to line', 'open terminal', 'save file') and an explanation of which specific versions were kept.

integrity.description: Ensure no original .talon files were deleted
integrity.visual_evidence: File Explorer or terminal directory listing shows that editor_commands.talon, general_commands.talon, and productivity_commands.talon all still exist in the directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent genuinely edited the files
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using a text editor (GUI or CLI) to manually inspect and modify the files, rather than running a pre-written script that bypasses the audit process.

integrity.description: Ensure the audit report is saved in the correct directory
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog, file explorer path, or terminal command confirms audit_report.txt is placed exactly in C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\community\user_commands\.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target directory is C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\community\user_commands. There are exactly 3 duplicate voice triggers to resolve: 'go to line', 'open terminal', and 'save file'. These conflicts are spread across 'editor_commands.talon', 'general_commands.talon', and 'productivity_commands.talon'. The final report must be saved as 'audit_report.txt' in the same directory.
